Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Verb phrase (intransitive verb + object complement); metaphorical action clause
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To waste time or effort on something pointless or unattainable
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Ma'na'nao i tiempo gi manmalågu' na cho'cho' ni ti mafa'tinas
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Fishing culture metaphor; reflects island livelihood where effort at sea must be purposeful
First Example Sentence: Mungnga chåda' guihan gi tasi; cho'gue i che'cho'-mu ni maolek.
Second Example Sentence: Ha chåda' guihan gi tasi anai ha tutuhon i proyekto ni ti para u mafa'tinas.
Third Example Sentence: Kao un chåda' guihan gi tasi esta, pat un li'e i rason?

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Verb phrase with prepositional complement; dynamic metaphor
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To adapt flexibly to life’s changing circumstances
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Ma adapta i taotao gi situasion siha gi lina'la'.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Rooted in communal dance traditions symbolizing harmony and resilience
First Example Sentence: Debi di un baila gi lina'la' maski guaha na pakpak i tiempo.
Second Example Sentence: I manåmko' ma nisisita un baila gi lina'la' para u fanmaolek.
Third Example Sentence: Ha baila gi lina'la' i familian-ña gi tiempo ni guaha pinatgon.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Transitive verb clause (ha + verb + direct object)
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To take responsibility or uphold family honor
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Chamorro value of inafa'maolek and family honor in clan-based society
First Example Sentence: Ha chule' i onra gi finakpo' i asuntu ni ma diskuti.
Second Example Sentence: I lider ha chule' i onra para i komunidad.
Third Example Sentence: Debi di un chule' i onra anai ma tungo' i che'cho'-mu.

Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: A deeply felt fear or anxiety
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Guaha na dangkolo na mahålang gi kurason.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Imagery of trapped animal symbolizing emotional turmoil
First Example Sentence: Guaha månnok gi halom kurason anai hu hungok i noticia.
Second Example Sentence: Ha na'guaha månnok gi halom kurason i situasion.
Third Example Sentence: Ti hu malågu' na guaha månnok gi halom kurason gi patgon.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Negative verb clause (ti + ha + verb + object)
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To ignore obvious wisdom or advice
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Ha ignora i konsehu ni klaru.
First Example Sentence: Ti ha li'e i tiningo' maski ma na'fanmanman gue'.
Second Example Sentence: Kao un ti ha li'e i tiningo' ni ma na'guaha?
Third Example Sentence: Ha ti ha li'e i tiningo' i patgon ni ma adahi.

Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Do not speak carelessly or disrespectfully
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Komprende i fino'-mu antes un håfa na sinangan.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Reflects Chamorro emphasis on respectful speech
First Example Sentence: Mungnga na'gasgas i fino'-mu gi me'nan i manåmko'.
Second Example Sentence: Ma sangåni gue' na mungnga na'gasgas i fino'-ña.
Third Example Sentence: Un hungok na mungnga na'gasgas i fino'-mu anai guaha bisita.

Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To lack clear thinking or good judgment
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Ti klaru i desision ni ha cho'gue.
First Example Sentence: Ha tåya' i hinengge anai ha cho'gue este na desision.
Second Example Sentence: Ma tungo' na ha tåya' i hinengge gi miting.
Third Example Sentence: Kao ha tåya' i hinengge pat guaha otro na rason?

Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Life is lively and dynamic in public spaces
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Mañåguaha i aktibidåt siha gi lugåt ni manma'åse'.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Reference to traditional marketplaces as social hubs
First Example Sentence: Guaha lina'la' gi palengke gi Sabadu gi aga'.
Second Example Sentence: Maolek na guaha lina'la' gi palengke ni manhånao i familian-ta.
Third Example Sentence: Un li'e na guaha lina'la' gi palengke anai manma'åse'.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Transitive verb clause with indirect object
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To respect and honor the elders’ authority
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Ha rispetu i manåmko' yan i autoridåt-da.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Land (tåno') symbolizing dignity and ancestral authority
First Example Sentence: Ha nå'e tåno' i manåmko' gi finakpo' i gima'.
Second Example Sentence: Debi di u nå'e tåno' i manåmko' i todu patgon.
Third Example Sentence: Ma sangåni ham na ha nå'e tåno' i manåmko' gi okkasion.

Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: An intense, piercing glare expressing anger or judgment
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Ha li'e ni dangkolo na binisita gi mata.
Register and Tone: Expressive, humorous or serious depending on tone
First Example Sentence: Ha na'guaha mata na'langet anai ma disgrasya i asuntu.
Second Example Sentence: Hu hungok i mata na'langet gi che'lu-hu anai hu håfa.
Third Example Sentence: Kao un li'e i mata na'langet ni ha nå'e gue'?
